Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Deposition Of Teresa Childers And Production Of Documents, And Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-15-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel Welting for plaintiff and moving party: Mary H. O'Guinn
Dennis P. McPherson for defendant and opposing party: Teresa Childers, Telephonic Appearance.
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification:
Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of Teresa Childers and the production of documents is granted. The committee described in the declaration submitted in opposition to the motion does not constitute a medical investigative committee as defined by Willits v. Superior Court (1993) 20 Cal.App.4th 90. Thus, any and all incident reports are not subject to the confidentiality provisions of Evidence Code Section 1157. The Court overrules the objections based on the attorney client and attorney work product privileges as to request #4. As drafted, request #5 seeks production of documents, some of which may be subject to a claim of privilege. The Court makes no rulings as to the propriety of withholding any responsive documents. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to scheduling the deposition at a mutually convenient time. The deposition is to be completed by August 31, 2005. The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
MODIFICATION: That production of documents to take place by August 29, 2005, deposition to be completed by September 20, 2005.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
